inline_review:
commit b6cacedf60194dcc5c87c32765edc7b50c65fc0d
Author: Abhishek Kumar <abhishek_sts8@yahoo.com>

mm: fix data race in __filemap_remove_folio / folio_mapping

This commit updates page_cache_delete() and folio_mapping() to use
WRITE_ONCE() and READ_ONCE() respectively when accessing folio->mapping.
This is intended to fix a KCSAN data race report and prevent compiler
store/load tearing.

Closes: https://syzkaller.appspot.com/bug?extid=606f94dfeaaa45124c90

> diff --git a/mm/filemap.c b/mm/filemap.c
> index 2b933a1da9bd..7aa4652d9b9f 100644
> --- a/mm/filemap.c
> +++ b/mm/filemap.c
> @@ -142,7 +142,7 @@ static void page_cache_delete(struct address_space *mapping,
>  	xas_store(&xas, shadow);
>  	xas_init_marks(&xas);
>  
> -	folio->mapping = NULL;
> +	WRITE_ONCE(folio->mapping, NULL);

Is this fix incomplete? Other page cache deletion paths like
page_cache_delete_batch() in mm/filemap.c, shmem_delete_from_page_cache()
in mm/shmem.c, and collapse_file() in mm/khugepaged.c still use a plain
store to set folio->mapping to NULL.

Since these functions also detach folios that can be accessed locklessly
by readers, won't KCSAN continue to report data races for those paths
unless they are also updated to use WRITE_ONCE()?

>  	/* Leave folio->index set: truncation lookup relies upon it */
>  	mapping->nrpages -= nr;
>  }

[ ... ]

> diff --git a/mm/util.c b/mm/util.c
> index f063fd4de1e8..fea9072cc19a 100644
> --- a/mm/util.c
> +++ b/mm/util.c
> @@ -729,7 +729,7 @@ struct address_space *folio_mapping(const struct folio *folio)
>  	if (unlikely(folio_test_swapcache(folio)))
>  		return swap_address_space(folio->swap);
>  
> -	mapping = folio->mapping;
> +	mapping = READ_ONCE(folio->mapping);

Should other lockless readers of folio->mapping also be updated?

Functions like folio_test_anon() and folio_test_ksm() in
include/linux/page-flags.h, as well as folio_raw_mapping() in
mm/internal.h, still perform plain loads.

For example, folio_test_anon() can be called locklessly by
damos_folio_filter_match() in mm/damon/ops-common.c. If these plain loads
run concurrently with the newly introduced WRITE_ONCE() here, KCSAN will
continue to report data races.

>  	if ((unsigned long)mapping & FOLIO_MAPPING_FLAGS)
>  		return NULL;
>
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"Incomplete data race fix: other page cache deletion paths still use plain stores to `folio->mapping`.","severity":"Medium","severity_explanation":"The patch's stated goal is to silence a KCSAN data race report and prevent compiler store/load tearing by converting the plain store of `folio->mapping` to `WRITE_ONCE()`. However, other functions that detach folios from the page cache—specifically `page_cache_delete_batch()` in `mm/filemap.c`, `shmem_delete_from_page_cache()` in `mm/shmem.c`, and `collapse_file()` in `mm/khugepaged.c`—perform the exact same `folio->mapping = NULL` operation using a plain store. Because lockless readers like `folio_mapping()` (which now uses `READ_ONCE()`) can still access these folios concurrently (e.g., via the LRU list or memory scanners), these plain stores will continue to race with the loads. KCSAN will continue to flag these as data races, and the theoretical risk of store tearing remains. The fix is incomplete without applying `WRITE_ONCE()` to these other writers."},{"problem":"Incomplete data race fix: other lockless readers of `folio->mapping` still use plain loads.","severity":"Medium","severity_explanation":"The commit addresses a data race where `folio->mapping` is read locklessly by `folio_mapping()`. However, it misses other inline accessors that also read `folio->mapping` and can be called locklessly. For example, `folio_test_anon()` and `folio_test_ksm()` in `include/linux/page-flags.h`, as well as `folio_raw_mapping()` in `mm/internal.h`, still perform plain loads (e.g., `((unsigned long)folio->mapping & FOLIO_MAPPING_ANON)`). These functions are demonstrably called on folios without holding the folio lock (for instance, `folio_test_anon()` is called locklessly by `damos_folio_filter_match()` in `mm/damon/ops-common.c` after acquiring a reference via `damon_get_folio()`). When these plain loads run concurrently with the newly introduced `WRITE_ONCE(folio->mapping, NULL)` in `page_cache_delete()`, KCSAN will continue to report data races, and the risk of load tearing is unmitigated. They must be updated to use `READ_ONCE(folio->mapping)`."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit b6cacedf60194dcc5c87c32765edc7b50c65fc0d

Since these functions also detach folios that can be accessed locklessly

>  	/* Leave folio->index set: truncation lookup relies upon it */
>  	mapping->nrpages -= nr;
>  }

[ ... ]

> diff --git a/mm/util.c b/mm/util.c
> index f063fd4de1e8..fea9072cc19a 100644
> --- a/mm/util.c
> +++ b/mm/util.c

Should other lockless readers of folio->mapping also be updated?

Functions like folio_test_anon() and folio_test_ksm() in

>  	if ((unsigned long)mapping & FOLIO_MAPPING_FLAGS)
>  		return NULL;
